sources.notes: Interactive easy version: find the maximum MEX among given ranges with a larger query budget.
Official easy solution removes contained ranges, detects the half containing position of 0, and queries only ranges that can contain it.

annotation.summary: Interactively discover the maximum MEX over a set of ranges in a hidden permutation, using the easy-version query budget.
annotation.constraints: The budget is about n/2 queries, so the solution can still query many filtered ranges but not all q naively.
annotation.core_idea: A range contained in another is useless because its MEX cannot exceed the larger range's MEX. After keeping only maximal ranges, query [1,n/2] to learn whether value 0 lies in the left or right half. Any range not containing that half cannot contain 0 and is useless; the remaining ranges are at most ceil(n/2), so query them and take the maximum.
annotation.complexity: O(n) preprocessing and O(n/2) interactive queries.
tricks: query only ranges that can contain zero

solution_variants.name: filtered-range-query
solution_variants.summary: Keep maximal ranges, determine the half containing value 0, and query every remaining candidate range.

tags.evidence: The solution is an interactive query strategy over hidden permutation ranges.
Ranges fully contained in other ranges are discarded before querying.
After filtering, the easy version directly queries all remaining candidate ranges.
